text: Hello, I'm sharing a case from call. Young person presented with a bowel perforation, peritonitis, and on scan you can see there's something in the mid abdomen, normal appendix, and it was presumed that this was probably a Meckel's.

The surgeon enters the left upper quadrant and inserts additional ports. Using a parambilical camera positioned on the left, the omentum is reflected upwards to visually assess the abdominal cavity and locate the pathology.
The surgeon identifies the inflamed section of the bowel with a visible perforation, corresponding to the location predicted by the CAT scan.
The surgeon places a suprapubic 12mm port, preparing for potential upsizing for specimen extraction, and begins careful blunt dissection of the inflamed bowel tissue.
The omentum is visibly attempting to seal off the perforation, with stool leaking from the tip of a structure resembling a Meckel's diverticulum, located on the anti-mesenteric surface of the bowel.
The surgeon carefully maneuvers the bowel segment from underneath the omentum, lifting it into view. The bowel is now clearly visible, elevated above the surrounding structures.

The surgeon addresses the perforation by breaking up loculations and freeing the bowel in preparation for resection. The bowel is run proximally and distally to check for additional pathology, focusing on the distal ileum approximately two feet from the ileocecal valve.
The surgeon prepares for a bowel resection, addressing the tension on the thick, friable mesentery by carefully dissecting and taking the mesentery first.
The surgeon performs a limited small bowel resection, dissecting proximal and distal to the area of the Meckel's diverticulum. The remaining mesentery is carefully taken distally.

The surgeon performs an extracorporeal side-to-side stapled anastomosis through a fan and seal incision around the twelve millimeter port in the suprapubic area. The common channel is closed with sutures, and the omentum is repositioned without leaving a drain.
I did keep the patient on antibiotics for a few days, but they did very well, and I hope you enjoyed.
